nodes.name: Google Sheets - Read Leads
nodes.notes: SETUP REQUIRED: Select the same 'Digital Artifacts Leads' Google Sheet from Workflow A

parameters.subject: Most small biz websites make these 3 mistakes
parameters.message:
=Hey {{ $json.name }},

I've been building websites for small businesses for a while now, and I keep seeing the same three things holding sites back:

1. Slow load times. If your site takes more than 3 seconds to load, most visitors leave before they even see what you offer. A quick fix? Compress your images and make sure your hosting isn't dragging you down.

2. Not mobile-friendly. Over 60% of web traffic in Australia comes from phones. If your site doesn't look great on mobile, you're losing more than half your audience.

3. No clear call-to-action. Every page should make it obvious what you want visitors to do next. "Get a Quote", "Book Now", "Call Us". Don't make people guess.

Which one of these resonates most with your business?

Cheers,
Isaiah
Digital Artifacts
https://digital-artifacts.vercel.app

parameters.subject: How a graphic designer went from no website to fully booked
parameters.message:
=Hey {{ $json.name }},

Wanted to share a quick story.

A graphic designer came to me last year with no online presence. She was relying entirely on word of mouth and Instagram DMs. Great work, but no way for new clients to find her or see her full portfolio.

We built her a clean, professional site with a portfolio showcase, contact form, and proper SEO. Within a few weeks she was getting enquiries through Google from people she'd never met.

The site loads fast, looks great on mobile, and she can update her portfolio whenever she wants. She told me it was the best investment she'd made for her business.

If you're curious what that looks like, here's her site: https://cee-essdesigns.com.au

Every business is different, but the pattern is the same. A solid website turns "word of mouth" into "found on Google".

Cheers,
Isaiah
Digital Artifacts

parameters.subject: The hidden cost of a cheap website
parameters.message:
=Hey {{ $json.name }},

I get it. DIY website builders like Wix and Squarespace seem like a bargain. $20 a month, drag and drop, done.

But here's what most people don't realise until later:

- You spend hours fiddling with templates that never quite look right
- Your site loads slowly because the builder adds bloated code
- You rank poorly on Google because the SEO options are limited
- When something breaks, you're on your own
- After 12 months of the subscription, you've spent more than a custom site would have cost. And you still don't love it.

A custom-built site costs more upfront, but you get something that's fast, looks professional, ranks well, and actually works for your business from day one.

Not saying DIY is always wrong. For some people it works great. But if your website is a key part of how customers find you, it's worth doing properly.

Just something to think about.

Cheers,
Isaiah
Digital Artifacts

parameters.message:

parameters.subject: Your website roadmap (free)

=Hey {{ $json.name }},

I wanted to offer you something I usually only do for paying clients.

If you're thinking about a website (new or redesign), I'll map out exactly what pages and features your site needs, what it would cost, and how long it'd take. For free.

Even if you don't end up going with me, you'll walk away with a clear plan you can take to anyone.

Interested? Just reply and I'll set it up.

Cheers,

parameters.subject: Still on your radar?
parameters.message:
